title: PACE package manual
code: if (!require("BiocManager")) install.packages("BiocManager") BiocManager::install("remotes") remotes::install_github("ecool50/PACE")

title: PACE package manual
text: PACE (Proximity-Associated Changes Expression) framework imaging-based spatial transcriptomics (Xenium CosMx) quantifies cell’s gene expression changes proximity specific neighbouring cell types. Rather asking genes spatially variable, PACE asks directed question: macrophage sits next tumour cells, genes go , much? PACE fits hierarchical negative binomial mixed model partial pooling across cell types, focal-neighbour relationship borrows strength others noisy pairs regularised towards consensus. per-cell contamination term absorbs short-range ambient signal leaks adjacent cells segmentation error, estimated neighbour effect reflects biology rather transcript misassignment.

title: PACE package manual
text: PACE analysis three stages, driven single call paceFit(): Neighbourhood construction. every cell, Gaussian biological kernel summarises abundance neighbouring cell type, short-range exponential technical kernel captures ambient contamination field. Model fitting. streaming penalised quasi-likelihood fit estimates, every gene every focal cell type, partially pooled slope neighbour type’s abundance, jointly per-cell contamination loading gene-wise overdispersions. neighbour slopes stabilised multivariate adaptive shrinkage. Interpretation. fit read three ways: shrunken neighbour slopes local false sign rate (neighbourSlopes()), per-gene variance decomposition (varianceDecomposition()), per-pair driver-score tables ranking genes mediate relationship (topDrivers()).

title: PACE package manual
text: package ships small worked example: spatial crop human breast cancer Xenium section (10x Genomics; nucleus segmentation, cell types annotated scClassify), centred tumour-macrophage interface. contains 7,898 cells across eight cell types (B cell, dendritic cell, endothelial, macrophage, myoepithelial, stromal, T cell, tumour) 278-gene panel, packaged SpatialExperiment. region reproduces macrophage reprogramming PACE identifies full section.
code: library(PACE) library(SpatialExperiment) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) library(tidyr)  spe <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "bc_xenium_subset.rds", package = "PACE")) spe #> class: SpatialExperiment  #> dim: 278 7898  #> metadata(0): #> assays(1): counts #> rownames(278): SEC11C DAPK3 ... NOSTRIN CD1C #> rowData names(0): #> colnames(7898): 442 444 ... 99842 99848 #> colData names(2): cellType sample_id #> reducedDimNames(0): #> mainExpName: NULL #> altExpNames(0): #> spatialCoords names(2) : x y #> imgData names(0): table(spe$cellType) #>  #>         B_Cell Dendritic_Cell    Endothelial     Macrophage  Myoepithelial  #>            330             32            499            591             47  #>        Stromal         T_Cell         Tumour  #>            681           1076           4642

title: PACE package manual
text: paceFit() convenience wrapper. result produced calling step explicitly, lets inspect fitted model re-run downstream layers (shrinkage, decomposition, drivers) without refitting: fit-construction primitives expose neighbourhood contamination field directly, inspection independently fit:
code: fit <- paceModel(spe, celltype_col = "cellType")   |>  # fit the mixed model        paceShrink()                                |>  # shrink neighbour slopes        paceDecompose(spe)                          |>  # variance decomposition        paceDrivers()                                   # per-pair driver scores kern <- buildNeighbourhood(spe, "cellType")   # Gaussian K_bio + technical K_tech W    <- ambientField(spe, "cellType")         # sparse E^tech contamination field anchorGenes(fit, spe)                         # per-cell-type contamination anchors

title: PACE package manual
text: plotDecomposition() shows, focal cell type, share expression variance attributable cell-type identity, spatial cell state (proximity effects), contamination (spillover), pooled genes. left panel full stacked bar; right zooms two small spatial components.  Beyond dominant cell-type identity component, macrophages, stromal myoepithelial cells carry largest spatial cell-state contributions. underlying per-gene table available varianceDecomposition(fit).
code: plotDecomposition(fit)

title: PACE package manual
text: plotPairHeatmap() gives percentage focal type’s total variance contributed spatial interaction neighbour, computed focal’s spatial share split across neighbours normalised Pratt attribution. Tumour neighbour drives strongest spatial signal across microenvironment.
code: plotPairHeatmap(fit)

title: PACE package manual
text: plotDrivers() ranks genes mediating relationship driver score (MCSD, combining shrunken slope, cell-type specificity, expression level), alongside per-gene single-frame decomposition. macrophage-tumour pair, top drivers tissue-resident marker MRC1 (CD206), reduced near tumour, lipid-associated marker APOC1, elevated near tumour.  shrunken slopes confirm opposing directions:
code: plotDrivers(fit, "Macrophage", "Tumour") neighbourSlopes(fit) |>   filter(focal == "Macrophage", neighbour == "Tumour",          gene %in% c("MRC1", "APOC1")) |>   select(gene, estimate_shrunk, lfsr) #>    gene estimate_shrunk         lfsr #> 1 APOC1       0.1299349 2.092082e-16 #> 2  MRC1      -0.1499909 1.354472e-14

title: PACE package manual
text: plotProximity() bins macrophage number tumour neighbours (within 30 um) shows gene’s raw counts per bin. genes zero-inflated box median sits zero bins, per-bin mean overlaid point make trend explicit. Macrophage MRC1 falls APOC1 rises tumour proximity.
code: plotProximity(fit, spe, c("MRC1", "APOC1"), "Macrophage", "Tumour")

title: PACE package manual
text: section PACE recovers coordinated shift macrophage state tumour interface: tissue-resident marker MRC1 (CD206) reduced lipid-associated, immunosuppressive marker APOC1 elevated macrophages near tumour cells. PACE separates proximity effect transcript contamination otherwise inflate tumour-marker signal macrophages, drivers promotes genuine macrophage genes rather tumour genes bleeding across cell boundaries.

what: Proximity-Associated Changes in Expression for Spatial Transcriptomics
title: Proximity-Associated Changes in Expression for Spatial Transcriptomics
text: hierarchical empirical Bayes framework proximity-associated changes expression — cell’s gene expression shifts near specific neighbouring cell types — imaging-based spatial transcriptomics (Xenium, CosMx).

title: Proximity-Associated Changes in Expression for Spatial Transcriptomics
text: PACE quantifies proximity specific neighbouring cell types reshapes cell’s gene expression. fits hierarchical negative binomial mixed models partial pooling across cell types, corrects transcript contamination adjacent cells per-cell ambient term, decomposes expression variance cell-type identity, spatial cell state, contamination, residual components. per-gene driver score ranks genes mediate focal–neighbour relationship. Input output follow SpatialExperiment conventions: single call paceFit() returns PACEFit object, read neighbourSlopes(), varianceDecomposition(), topDrivers(). See package vignette worked breast cancer example.

title: Proximity-Associated Changes in Expression for Spatial Transcriptomics
text: PACE requires C++ compiler (via Rcpp/RcppEigen) contamination-correcting solver. like --date features, install development version GitHub.
code: # install.packages("BiocManager") BiocManager::install("remotes") remotes::install_github("ecool50/PACE") library(PACE)

what: Submitting an issue or feature request
title: Proximity-Associated Changes in Expression for Spatial Transcriptomics
text: PACE still active development. greatly appreciate feedback related package. R package related issues raised . general questions feedback, please contact us directly via ewil3501@uni.sydney.edu.au.

title: PACE: Proximity-Associated Changes in Expression — PACE-package
text: PACE quantifies cell's gene expression changes proximity specific neighbouring cell types imaging-based spatial transcriptomics. fits hierarchical negative binomial mixed models partial pooling across cell types, corrects transcript contamination adjacent cells per-cell ambient term, decomposes expression variance cell-type identity, spatial cell state, contamination, residual components.

title: PACE: Proximity-Associated Changes in Expression — PACE-package

title: PACE: Proximity-Associated Changes in Expression — PACE-package
text: Maintainer: Elijah Willie ewillie0004@gmail.com Authors: Shreya Rajesh Rao John Ormerod Ellis Patrick

what: PACEFit: a fitted PACE model — PACEFit
title: PACEFit: a fitted PACE model — PACEFit
text: S4 container result PACE fit. holds raw penalised quasi-likelihood solver state together three interpretable output layers: shrunken per-(gene, focal, neighbour) proximity slopes, per-gene variance decomposition, per-pair driver tables. Construct one paceFit(); read neighbourSlopes(), varianceDecomposition(), topDrivers().

title: PACEFit: a fitted PACE model — PACEFit
code: # S4 method for class 'PACEFit' show(object)

title: PACEFit: a fitted PACE model — PACEFit
text: fit raw streaming PQL solver state (fixed random effects, per-cell contamination loadings, gene-wise overdispersions, variance components). neighbourSlopes data frame shrunken proximity coefficients, one row per (gene, focal cell type, neighbour cell type, condition term), raw estimate, shrunken estimate, local false sign rate (lfsr). varianceDecomposition list per-gene, per-focal variance decomposition tables (cell-type identity, spatial cell state, contamination, residual; , condition cohorts, responder spatial block). topDrivers list per-pair driver-score tables ranking genes mediate focal-neighbour spatial relationship. cellTypes cell types modelled, fitting order. params fitting parameters actually used (bandwidths, contamination dispersion settings, condition column, ). context working frame fixed-effect design retained fit downstream stages (paceShrink(), paceDecompose(), paceDrivers()) can run without refitting.

what: Build the ambient contamination field — ambientField
title: Build the ambient contamination field — ambientField
text: Returns sparse cross-cell-type ambient weight matrix defines per-cell contamination term (short-range technical field cell receives heterotypic neighbours).

title: Build the ambient contamination field — ambientField
code: ambientField(object, ...)  # S4 method for class 'SpatialExperiment' ambientField(   object,   celltype_col,   image_col = NULL,   h_tech = 5,   assay_name = "counts",   ... )

title: Build the ambient contamination field — ambientField
text: object SpatialExperiment::SpatialExperiment. ... Passed ambient-field builder. celltype_col colData column cell-type annotation. image_col Optional colData column grouping cells images. h_tech Technical bandwidth micrometres (default 5). assay_name Counts assay name (default "counts").

title: Build the ambient contamination field — ambientField
text: ambient-field object (sparse weight matrix image index).

what: Contamination anchor genes per cell type — anchorGenes
title: Contamination anchor genes per cell type — anchorGenes
text: Returns, cell type, genes used negative-control anchors identifying per-cell contamination loading: genes owned another cell type (high mean expression ) near-absent focal type. Anchors recomputed selection fit, ranked expression owning cell type. source manuscript's supplementary anchor-gene tables.

title: Contamination anchor genes per cell type — anchorGenes
code: anchorGenes(object, spe, top_n = 10L, assay_name = "counts")

title: Contamination anchor genes per cell type — anchorGenes
text: object PACEFit (supplies cell-type image columns used fit time). spe SpatialExperiment::SpatialExperiment fitted. top_n Number top anchors list per cell type (default 10). assay_name Counts assay name (default "counts").

title: Contamination anchor genes per cell type — anchorGenes
text: data frame one row per cell type: cellType, n_anchors (total anchors), top_anchors (top_n strongest, comma-separated).

title: Contamination anchor genes per cell type — anchorGenes
code: if (FALSE)  anchorGenes(fit, spe)  # \dontrun{}

title: Build the neighbourhood kernels — buildNeighbourhood
text: Returns per-cell kernel-weighted neighbour abundances: Gaussian biological kernel K_bio (used proximity coefficients) short-range exponential technical kernel K_tech. Useful inspecting neighbourhood independently fit.

title: Build the neighbourhood kernels — buildNeighbourhood
code: buildNeighbourhood(object, ...)  # S4 method for class 'SpatialExperiment' buildNeighbourhood(   object,   celltype_col,   h_bio = 30,   h_tech = 5,   eps = NULL,   ... )

title: Build the neighbourhood kernels — buildNeighbourhood
text: object SpatialExperiment::SpatialExperiment. ... Passed kernel builder. celltype_col colData column cell-type annotation. h_bio, h_tech Bandwidths micrometres (defaults 30, 5). eps Truncation radius; defaults 3 * h_bio.

title: Build the neighbourhood kernels — buildNeighbourhood
text: list K_bio K_tech (cells x cell types).

title: Shrunken neighbour proximity slopes — neighbourSlopes

title: Variance decomposition — paceDecompose
text: Partitions per-gene expression variance cell-type identity, spatial cell state, contamination, residual (plus responder block condition cohorts), populating varianceDecomposition(). Needs fitted object plus SpatialExperiment used paceModel() (read counts).

title: Variance decomposition — paceDecompose
code: paceDecompose(object, ...)  # S4 method for class 'PACEFit' paceDecompose(object, spe, ...)

title: Variance decomposition — paceDecompose
text: object PACEFit paceModel(). ... Unused. spe SpatialExperiment::SpatialExperiment fitted.

title: Variance decomposition — paceDecompose
text: PACEFit variance decomposition added.

title: Per-pair driver scores — paceDrivers
text: Ranks genes mediating focal-neighbour relationship driver score, populating topDrivers(). Requires paceShrink() paceDecompose() run first.

title: Per-pair driver scores — paceDrivers
code: paceDrivers(object, ...)  # S4 method for class 'PACEFit' paceDrivers(object, pairs = NULL, ...)

title: Per-pair driver scores — paceDrivers
text: object PACEFit shrunken slopes decomposition. ... Unused. pairs Optional list focal-neighbour pairs score; NULL scores pairs.

title: Per-pair driver scores — paceDrivers
text: PACEFit driver tables added.

what: Fit a PACE model to a SpatialExperiment — paceFit
title: Fit a PACE model to a SpatialExperiment — paceFit
text: paceFit() runs full pipeline one call: paceModel() -> paceShrink() -> paceDecompose() -> paceDrivers(). step--step control (inspecting fitted model, re-running downstream without refitting), call stages directly.

title: Fit a PACE model to a SpatialExperiment — paceFit
code: paceFit(object, ...)  # S4 method for class 'SpatialExperiment' paceFit(object, ..., pairs = NULL)

title: Fit a PACE model to a SpatialExperiment — paceFit
text: object SpatialExperiment::SpatialExperiment. ... Arguments passed paceModel() (celltype_col, image_col, contamination, dispersion, ...). pairs Optional list focal-neighbour pairs driver tables.

title: Fit a PACE model to a SpatialExperiment — paceFit
text: fully populated PACEFit.

title: Fit a PACE model to a SpatialExperiment — paceFit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fit <- paceFit(spe, celltype_col = "cellType") neighbourSlopes(fit) } # }

title: Fit the PACE model — paceModel
text: paceModel() builds biological technical neighbourhood kernels spatial coordinates cell-type labels fits hierarchical negative binomial mixed model streaming penalised quasi-likelihood (per-cell contamination correction). returns PACEFit carrying fitted model; reporting layers added paceShrink(), paceDecompose(), paceDrivers() (paceFit()).

title: Fit the PACE model — paceModel
code: paceModel(object, ...)  # S4 method for class 'SpatialExperiment' paceModel(   object,   celltype_col,   image_col = NULL,   condition_col = NULL,   assay_name = "counts",   h_bio = 30,   h_tech = 5,   contamination = c("percell_hc", "none"),   dispersion = c("nb1", "nb2"),   kernel_per_image = FALSE,   image_re = c("none", "intercept", "slopes", "condition_slopes"),   types = NULL,   resp_term = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Fit the PACE model — paceModel
text: object SpatialExperiment::SpatialExperiment counts assay two-dimensional spatial coordinates. ... arguments passed streaming fitter (n_iter, threads, drop_sparse_neff, within_image, edge_correct, data_informed_tau, tau_shrinkage, ...). celltype_col colData column discrete cell-type annotation. image_col Optional colData column grouping cells images/samples. NULL (default) treats object one section. condition_col Optional colData column binary condition; enables responder spatial block multi-sample cohorts. assay_name Counts assay name (default "counts"). h_bio, h_tech Biological technical kernel bandwidths micrometres (defaults 30 5). contamination "percell_hc" (default) "none". dispersion "nb1" (default) "nb2". kernel_per_image TRUE, neighbourhoods built within image. image_re Second image-level random-effect block: "none", "intercept", "slopes", "condition_slopes". types Cell types order index random-effect blocks. NULL (default) uses every observed type alphabetical order; pass explicit vector reproduce locked fit, whose block order caller chose. resp_term Responder interaction term prefix condition cohorts; NULL derived condition_col. verbose Whether print progress.

title: Fit the PACE model — paceModel
text: PACEFit fitted model (reporting layers empty).

title: Fit the PACE model — paceModel
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fit <- paceModel(spe, celltype_col = "cellType") |>          paceShrink() |> paceDecompose(spe) |> paceDrivers() } # }

title: Pairwise spatial-percent heatmap — plotPairHeatmap
text: Reproduces manuscript pairwise heatmap: cell percentage focal type's total variance contributed spatial interaction neighbour, computed focal's spatial share split across neighbours -diagonal-renormalised Pratt attribution.

title: Pairwise spatial-percent heatmap — plotPairHeatmap
code: plotPairHeatmap(object, block = c("spatial", "responder"), title = NULL)

title: Pairwise spatial-percent heatmap — plotPairHeatmap
text: object PACEFit. block spatial block map: "spatial" (default, baseline spatial cell state) "responder" (responder--proximity block, condition cohorts). title Plot title; sensible default chosen per block.

title: Pairwise spatial-percent heatmap — plotPairHeatmap
text: ggplot object.

title: Pairwise spatial-percent heatmap — plotPairHeatmap
code: if (FALSE)  plotPairHeatmap(fit); plotPairHeatmap(fit, block = "responder")  # \dontrun{} #> Error: object 'fit' not found

what: Expression versus number of neighbours (per-bin boxplot) — plotProximity
title: Expression versus number of neighbours (per-bin boxplot) — plotProximity
text: Reproduces manuscript proximity figure (verbatim style port density_bin_boxplot analysis helpers): focal cell number neighbour-type cells within radius micrometres counted binned, gene's raw counts drawn boxplot per bin per-bin mean overlaid point. genes zero-inflated box median sits zero bins, mean point carries trend.

title: Expression versus number of neighbours (per-bin boxplot) — plotProximity
code: plotProximity(   object,   spe,   genes,   focal,   neighbour,   radius = 30,   breaks = c(0, 2, 4, 6, 8, Inf),   box_colour = "#4F8B5E",   assay_name = "counts" )

title: Expression versus number of neighbours (per-bin boxplot) — plotProximity
text: object PACEFit (supplies cell-type column used fit time). spe SpatialExperiment::SpatialExperiment fitted. genes Character vector genes (shown facets). focal, neighbour Focal neighbour cell types. radius Neighbour search radius micrometres (default 30). breaks Neighbour-count bin breaks (default c(0, 2, 4, 6, 8, Inf)). box_colour Box mean-point colour. assay_name Counts assay name (default "counts").

title: Expression versus number of neighbours (per-bin boxplot) — plotProximity
text: ggplot object.

title: Expression versus number of neighbours (per-bin boxplot) — plotProximity
code: if (FALSE)  plotProximity(fit, spe, c("MRC1", "APOC1"), "Macrophage", "Tumour")  # \dontrun{}

title: Per-pair driver tables — topDrivers
text: Returns driver-score tables ranking genes mediate focal-neighbour spatial relationship.

title: Per-pair driver tables — topDrivers
code: topDrivers(object, ...)  # S4 method for class 'PACEFit' topDrivers(object, ...)

title: Per-pair driver tables — topDrivers
text: object PACEFit. ... Unused.

title: Per-pair driver tables — topDrivers
text: list per-pair driver tables.

title: Per-pair driver tables — topDrivers
code: if (FALSE)  topDrivers(fit)  # \dontrun{}

title: Per-gene variance decomposition — varianceDecomposition
text: Returns per-gene, per-focal variance decomposition. default observed single-frame decomposition (cell-type identity, spatial cell state, contamination, residual; responder spatial block condition cohorts) returned; set = "blocks" underlying link-scale block tables.

title: Per-gene variance decomposition — varianceDecomposition
code: varianceDecomposition(object, ...)  # S4 method for class 'PACEFit' varianceDecomposition(object, which = c("perGene", "blocks"), ...)

title: Per-gene variance decomposition — varianceDecomposition
text: object PACEFit. ... Unused. Either "perGene" (default, observed single-frame table) "blocks" (raw link-scale decomposition).

title: Per-gene variance decomposition — varianceDecomposition
text: data frame ("perGene") list ("blocks").

title: Per-gene variance decomposition — varianceDecomposition
code: if (FALSE)  varianceDecomposition(fit)  # \dontrun{}

title: PACE 0.99.0
text: Initial package: paceFit() SpatialExperiment input, PACEFit S4 class, neighbourSlopes(), varianceDecomposition(), topDrivers() accessors, wrapping locked PACE statistical engine.
